name: Goose
description: Open-source, on-machine AI agent (CLI and desktop, Rust) under the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation; multi-provider and MCP-native.

developer.comment: Created by Block Open Source ('codename goose'); contributed to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) in Dec 2025, which now stewards the project.

first-release.comment: Block introduced codename goose as an open-source CLI + desktop agent in January 2025.

status.comment: Actively developed; ~46k GitHub stars, frequent releases. Checked 2026-06-05.

specialization.comment: Positioned as a general-purpose on-machine agent (code, automation, research, data analysis), not narrowed to a niche.

website.comment: Official site redirects to goose-docs.ai as of 2026.

interface.comment: Ships as a terminal CLI and a native desktop app (macOS, Linux, Windows), plus an embeddable API.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal (CLI) and as a standalone desktop application; not an IDE plugin.

open-source.comment: Agent source is Apache-2.0 licensed and on GitHub.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Fundamentally a CLI tool plus a standalone desktop app; not an extension to another editor.

default-model-provider.comment: Provider-agnostic: 15+ providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama, OpenRouter, Azure, Bedrock, etc.). No single default vendor; user configures the model.

model-choice.comment: User selects provider and model freely across 15+ supported providers.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Users supply their own API keys (secrets.yaml); Goose has no vendor billing of its own.

local-model-support.comment: Runs against local models via Ollama (and other OpenAI-compatible local endpoints).

multi-file-editing.comment: Edits and creates files and runs code across a project as part of multi-step tasks.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains steps autonomously (edit, execute, test) toward a goal; autonomous permission mode runs without per-step approval.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Built-in Developer extension lets it run shell commands and execute code as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: MCP-native; extensions are MCP servers (70+ documented). Goose co-founders contributed MCP itself to AAIF.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Supports spawning independent subagents and sub-recipes to handle tasks in parallel (code review, research, file processing).

codebase-indexing.comment: No built-in semantic index/embeddings store documented; reads files on demand and via extensions rather than maintaining a repo embedding index.

planning-mode.comment: Explicit planning mode with a configurable planner model (GOOSE_PLANNER_PROVIDER / GOOSE_PLANNER_MODEL).

multimodal-input.comment: Desktop app accepts image input and works with vision-capable models; roadmap notes further vision improvements for local inference, so coverage depends on the chosen model. Checked 2026-06-05.

browser-computer-use.comment: Can drive a real browser via the Playwright/Computer-Controller MCP extensions (navigate, click, screenshot) rather than only fetching text.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Per the official subagents guide, you ask Goose to delegate in natural language and 'Goose automatically decides when to spawn subagents and handles their lifecycle' — the model chooses whether and how many subagents to fan out (e.g. 'build a task app doing these 3 tasks in parallel' → Goose spawns three independent instances) rather than the user launching each one via a fixed command. Model-driven delegation, though prompt-encouraged rather than fully unsolicited. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive chat sessions plus supervised (manual/smart approval) and fully autonomous permission modes. No tab inline completion; runs in the focused session rather than detached cloud workers.

inline-completion.comment: CLI/desktop chat agent; no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Recipes can be run headlessly (goose run --recipe) in CI/scheduled jobs, allowing non-interactive background execution.

persistent-memory.comment: Memory Extension stores cross-session memory in ~/.goose/memory (local) or global config; .goosehints provide static persistent project/user context.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No built-in checkpoint/snapshot-restore of the working tree documented; reverting relies on git or manual undo. Checked 2026-06-05.

git-integration.comment: Runs git via shell commands and via a GitHub MCP extension; no special native git UI beyond command execution.

pr-creation.comment: Can open/update pull requests through the GitHub MCP extension or gh CLI commands.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports .goosehints instruction files (project, global, and local scopes).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Highly extensible via MCP extensions, custom slash commands, and shareable recipes.

permission-model.comment: Configurable permission modes: Autonomous (auto-run), Manual Approval (ask), Smart Approval (risk-based), Chat Only; also offers a sandbox mode and prompt-injection detection.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine; open-source and can point at self-hosted/local model endpoints.

privacy-mode.comment: On-machine agent with BYOK; code is sent only to the LLM provider the user chooses (or a local model), so training-data exposure is governed by that provider, not by Goose.

enterprise-controls.comment: Self-hosted open-source tool with no built-in SSO/RBAC/audit-log/VPC governance layer of its own; such controls would come from the chosen model provider or surrounding infra. Checked 2026-06-05.

pricing-model.comment: The agent is free and open-source; users pay only their own LLM provider via BYOK (or run a free local model).

free-tier.comment: The tool itself is fully free; only model/API usage costs apply.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free, open-source agent with no subscription; 0 per the guideline since the free tier covers core use (model costs are separate).

maturity.comment: GA, ~46k stars, regular releases, now stewarded by the Linux Foundation AAIF; still evolving quickly but widely used and stable.

community-size.comment: ~46k GitHub stars, active Discord and recipe community; comparable to other large OSS agents like Aider.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: 70+ documented MCP extensions plus a community recipe cookbook; broad MCP ecosystem.

memory-scopes.comment: .goosehints and Memory Extension support a global/user scope (~/.goosehints, global memory) and a per-project scope; no enterprise-policy or per-folder scoping documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: .goosehints are loaded in full on every request (always-on); no path/glob or file-type conditional activation documented.

auto-memory.comment: Memory Extension can automatically suggest saving recurring knowledge across sessions; it is an optional extension that can be toggled off.

skill-support.comment: Recipes are reusable, packaged task capabilities (instructions + extensions + parameters + sub-recipes) loaded on demand; also supports custom slash commands.

skill-invocation.comment: Recipes can be launched explicitly by the user (goose run --recipe / shareable URLs) and sub-recipes can be invoked by the agent itself within a workflow.

always-on-surface.comment: Recipes are loaded fully when invoked rather than kept entirely in context; closest fit is progressive-disclosure description-only. Some interpretation involved.

skill-distribution.comment: Recipes are shared via a community Recipe Cookbook, a GitHub recipe repo (GOOSE_RECIPE_GITHUB_REPO), shareable URLs, and hand-copied YAML files.

sandboxing-technology.comment: By default (and always on the CLI) Goose executes tools with the user's full privileges, so no isolation (none). Goose Desktop offers an opt-in macOS sandbox (GOOSE_SANDBOX=true) built on Apple sandbox-exec / Seatbelt (os-sandbox) plus an egress proxy; it is disabled by default and macOS-only. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Permissions are set per tool (always_allow / ask_before / never_allow) and support wildcard patterns over tool names (e.g. */read_*, */write_*) for prefix-style allowlisting. Smart Approval distinguishes read-only operations (file reads, SELECT) from state-changing ones, gating reads and writes differently. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: Explicit user permissions in permission.yaml take precedence over Smart Approval's automated decisions, and never_allow blocks a tool regardless of read-only annotations. The engine is allow/ask/deny per tool name pattern with no documented specificity-based re-override or user-controlled ordering, so deny-overrides is the highest rung reached. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: Beyond picking a mode (preset), users author durable per-tool allow/ask/deny rules persisted in permission.yaml (declarative-rules). No code-level permission hook/policy-script decision point documented, so not programmable. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No default deny patterns for secrets/.env in the standard CLI or Desktop config. Protection of ~/.ssh, shell rc files, and goose config exists only inside the opt-in macOS sandbox (GOOSE_SANDBOX_PROTECT_FILES), which is disabled by default, so out-of-the-box there is no built-in secret gating. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: User permission decisions (always_allow / never_allow) are durably persisted in permission.yaml and survive across sessions, so allowed tools are not re-prompted on restart. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Autonomous mode (GooseMode::Auto) allows every tool call without approval prompts for hands-off runs; it is in fact the default mode. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: By default outbound network is unrestricted (tools run with full user network access). The opt-in macOS Desktop sandbox adds an egress proxy that denies direct network access and filters traffic via allow/block lists (~/.config/goose/sandbox/blocked.txt, optional LaunchDarkly enterprise policy), making egress configurable when enabled. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box Goose runs in Autonomous mode, executing file edits and shell commands without asking for per-action approval. Checked 2026-06-12.
